stem: A climate monitoring study measures daily temperature readings for 40 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±0.5°C of the calibration standard. The sample standard deviation is 1.25 °C (sample variance = 1.57). A second measurement method on 20 samples gives a sample variance of 1.06 (SD = 1.03 °C). The two methods may have different measurement precision.  Estimate the true population standard deviation of daily temperature readings and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Method 1: s=1.25, 95% CI for σ = [1.02, 1.61]. Method 2: s=1.03, 95% CI for σ = [0.78, 1.5]. Combined interval: [0.78, 1.61]. Point estimate: 1.15 °C.

solution_steps: Combined interval accounting for method uncertainty: [0.78, 1.61].
common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (1.25) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
Averaging the two sample variances without considering they may represent different measurement precisions
